title: Comparison operators should not be virtual
description: Comparison operators should not be virtualAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellcppcoreguidelinespitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoMaking a comparison operatorvirtualimplies that you want to compare objects of different types by overridingoperator==, for instance, in a subclass to compare instances of the base class with instances of the subclass. But polymorphic comparison operators are very difficult to get right, and are actually questionable in concept. After all, can two objects with only a few common members really be equal?This rule raises issues onvirtualcomparison operators.Noncompliant code examplestruct Foo { virtual bool operator==(const Foo &other) const; // Noncompliant virtual bool operator!=(const Foo &other) const; // Noncompliant };Compliant sol
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Comparison operators should not be virtual.
